claim: ECB-Fed-BOJ feedback loop from AI adoption asymmetry creates self-reinforcing divergence through currency, inflation, and capital flow channels
needs: Corroboration from additional agents; isolation of AI adoption asymmetry as primary driver vs demographics, fiscal stance, and productivity differentials; testable near-term predictions

claim: QT is roughly half as effective as Fed models suggest due to AI equity FCI offset of ~100-150bp
needs: Public FCI decomposition methodology; independent corroboration; verification that Fed staff forecasts systematically overstate restrictiveness

claim: Insurance capital amplification channel producing $15-40B charges from private credit downgrades via NAIC RBC step-function
needs: Validated credit migration rate assumptions for AI-disruption scenarios; confirmed insurance private credit allocation data; stress test modeling of capital charge cascades

claim: PIK toggle activation rate at 8-12% vs 2-4% historical serves as observable Minsky speculative-to-Ponzi transition indicator
needs: Comprehensive PIK activation data across both BDC filings and opaque non-traded vehicles; clear threshold that distinguishes speculative from Ponzi classification

claim: Nifty Fifty (1968-1974) as the primary analogue for concentration premium with 800-1200bp annual underperformance for 7-10 years post-peak
needs: Reconciliation of materially different macro backdrop (1970s stagflation vs current moderate inflation); adjustment for different name composition (consumer staples/tech hardware vs AI/cloud); larger N of concentration premium episodes

claim: Conglomerate era (1960s-1980s) as ROIC dilution analogue where ITT revenue grew 15x with declining per-share value
needs: Resolution of self-contradiction: if governance context is the argument for superiority, yet governance failed to alter outcomes for 8-15 years, the analogue predicts the same slow grind as Japan without changing the conclusion

claim: Narrative validation window opens Q3 2026-Q2 2027 based on cross-analogue 3-5 year patience period pattern
needs: Confirmation that self-funded capex does not extend patience period beyond ±2-3 quarter uncertainty; specific observable metrics that would confirm Stage 2 activation; resolution of AI revenue measurement ambiguity

claim: Natural gas curve seasonality compression of $0.30-0.50/MMBtu from data center baseload demand at PJM-adjacent delivery points
needs: Signal-to-noise ratio improvement as data center demand grows; isolation from weather-driven volatility ($1-2/MMBtu swings); confirmation from multiple delivery points

claim: Semi-liquid private credit vehicles ($200-300B) face auction-rate-securities-style coordination failure if retail investors face first-time gates
needs: Narrower vehicle size estimates; behavioral data on retail investor response to first-time gates; analysis of structural differences from 2008 ARS investor base

claim: AI synergy EBITDA addbacks at 15-25% render reported 5-6x leverage effectively 7-9x on cash-basis EBITDA
needs: Independent corroboration from other agents; systematic data collection on AI synergy addback prevalence and growth; rating agency treatment and response
